Title: The ETHYLENE RESPONSE FACTOR6-GRETCHEN HAGEN3.5 module regulates rooting and heat tolerance in Dimocarpus longan

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: IAA-amino acid conjugates (IAA-Asp; IAA-Glu; IAA-Phe; IAA-Leu)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Conjugates free IAA to amino acids, reducing the free IAA pool and increasing IAA-amino acid conjugates.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GH3 acyl acid amido synthetase family
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: overexpression; RNAi; RT-qPCR; metabolite profiling of IAA; heat stress phenotyping; dual-luciferase assay; GUS reporter assay; yeast one-hybrid; DAP-seq
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expression decreased in DlERF6-OE roots and increased in DlERF6-RNAi roots under control and heat treatments
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DlGH3.5 is a direct target of DlERF6, whose promoter is bound by DlERF6 at a GCC-box. 2. Overexpression of DlGH3.5 reduced hairy root number and length, reduced free IAA content, and increased ROS accumulation. 3. DlGH3.5 overexpression decreased heat tolerance, whereas DlGH3.5 RNAi increased IAA, antioxidant enzyme activities, and flavonoid/lignin metabolites under heat stress.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduces free IAA accumulation and negatively regulates hairy root growth and heat tolerance.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter for overexpression in hairy roots; native DlGH3.5 promoter used in reporter assays
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Heat stress (42°C, 3 d); IAA treatment context through upstream regulation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Auxin supports root growth and contributes to heat tolerance through ROS homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression causes fewer and shorter hairy roots, lower heat tolerance, higher ROS, and reduced antioxidant capacity
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Gene silencing/editing of DlGH3.5 could be used to increase free IAA, improve root system development, and enhance heat tolerance in longan
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: DlGH3.5 also negatively affects flavonoid and lignin metabolite accumulation under heat stress.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: IAA (indole-3-acetic acid); flavonoid metabolites; lignin metabolites
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: AP2/ERF family transcription factor with conserved AP2 domain
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: overexpression; RNAi; RT-qPCR; heat stress phenotyping; IAA and IAA-amino acid metabolite profiling; ROS and antioxidant enzyme assays; RNA-seq; untargeted metabolomics; DAP-seq; dual-luciferase assay; GUS reporter assay; yeast one-hybrid
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed across tissues, dominantly in stems and roots, followed by seeds and young fruits; strongly induced in roots by heat and IAA treatment
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in roots by heat stress and IAA treatment
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter for overexpression in hairy roots
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes free IAA accumulation, enhances flavonoid and lignin metabolite accumulation, improves ROS scavenging, increases hairy root growth, and enhances heat tolerance.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Auxin homeostasis; ROS scavenging; secondary metabolite biosynthesis; heat-stress response
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DlERF6 is a nucleus-localized ERF transcription factor induced by heat stress and IAA in longan roots. 2. DlERF6 overexpression increased hairy root number and length and enhanced heat tolerance, whereas RNAi had opposite effects. 3. DlERF6 directly binds the GCC-box in the DlGH3.5 promoter and represses DlGH3.5 transcription, thereby increasing free IAA and reducing IAA-amino acid conjugates. 4. DlERF6 promotes ROS scavenging through higher antioxidant enzyme activities and is associated with increased flavonoid and lignin metabolites under heat stress.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses DlGH3.5; positively associated with upregulation of auxin biosynthesis genes and antioxidant-related genes; positively associated with flavonoid/lignin biosynthetic genes under heat stress
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression of DlGH3.5 via direct promoter binding to GCC-box
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent positive regulator that is decisive for maintaining auxin homeostasis and antioxidant metabolite accumulation under heat stress
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Heat stress (42°C); exogenous IAA treatment (1 mg/L)
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq BioProject PRJNA1102508; DAP-seq; untargeted metabolomics; ubiquitinome profiling
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Potential auxin-associated feedforward/homeostatic regulation inferred, but not directly established
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No clear growth penalty reported; instead overexpression increased root growth and heat tolerance in the tested system
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for engineering stronger root systems and improved heat tolerance in longan, potentially via overexpression or modulation of the DlERF6-DlGH3.5 module
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: DAP-seq identified GCC-box binding motif; ubiquitination, antioxidant pathways, auxin metabolism, flavonoid biosynthesis, and lignin biosynthesis were all altered in DlERF6-OE roots under heat stress.
